summary:
• Iran's seizures and attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz are creating critical disruption to global oil flows, with traffic grinding to a halt and threatening the supply cushion for Asian markets.

• Middle East geopolitical tensions centered on Iran are driving oil prices toward $95/barrel equilibrium, creating supply route risks that extend beyond the Western Hemisphere and impact global energy markets.

• Exploration setbacks like Equinor's failed PL 120 well and reliance on LNG restart announcements signal tightening supply conditions that could pressure Western Hemisphere producers to maximize output amid elevated global crude prices.

articles.title: Equinor ends PL 120 drilling near Visund field with no discovery
articles.description: Equinor and its co-licensees have concluded drilling operations at the “Skoll” and “Hati” prospects located east of the Visund field in the Norwegian North Sea, without discovering any hydrocarbons.
